name: Generic Develop / Test / Finalize Workflow
description: Deliverable-agnostic single-developer process workflow. It expresses HOW any assignment is completed, not WHAT to build. The LLM only writes source, test, and documentation files in DEVELOP. The engine runs the full Jest suite (TEST) and performs every git commit plus working-tree hygiene (FINALIZE) deterministically via onExitCommands. The WHAT comes entirely from each assignment's taskPrompt, so this same workflow drives every assignment (module, extension, docs) without deliverable-specific states, prompts, or gates. Workflow-driven counterpart to converter-ad-hoc for A/B comparison: the only intended difference between the two arms is who runs the deterministic steps (engine here, LLM in ad-hoc).

DEVELOP.name: Develop
DEVELOP.description: The developer completes the current assignment: write or edit whatever source, test, and/or documentation files the assignment requires. No git, no jest, no build tools. The engine runs the tests and performs all commits in the following states.
DEVELOP.prompt:
You are working in the project at {{projectPath}}, a git repository that already has a Jest + TypeScript setup in place (package.json, jest.config.cjs, tsconfig.json). Do NOT create or modify that harness configuration.

Complete ONLY the work described under 'Task prompt' below. Write or edit the source, test, and/or documentation files the assignment asks for, in {{projectPath}}.

IMPORTANT. The workflow engine performs all process steps for you:
  - Do NOT run git or make any commits.
  - Do NOT run jest or any tests. The engine runs the full suite next and will route this work back to you if any test fails.
  - Do NOT run tsc or other build tools.
Just make the file changes the assignment requires and report what you wrote.

params.message: DEVELOP complete for task {{taskId}} -- handing to TEST

TEST.name: Test
TEST.description: Engine-only gate (empty prompt: no LLM turn). Runs the full Jest suite deterministically and captures its console output. A failing suite blocks the transition and routes back to DEVELOP for rework.

onExitCommands.reason: Deterministic test gate. Runs the full Jest suite and captures its console output to test_output.txt. A non-zero exit (a failing test) blocks the transition and self-loops to DEVELOP for rework. --passWithNoTests keeps the gate deliverable-agnostic: an assignment that ships no new tests (for example a docs-only assignment) still passes on the existing committed suite.

params.message: TEST gate passed for task {{taskId}} -- handing to FINALIZE

FINALIZE.name: Finalize
FINALIZE.description: Engine-only step (empty prompt: no LLM turn). Commits every deliverable this assignment produced, including the captured test output, then runs working-tree hygiene and gates on a clean tree.

onExitCommands.command: git add -A && (git diff --cached --quiet || git commit -m 'chore: commit deliverables for {{taskId}}')
onExitCommands.reason: Commit every deliverable this assignment produced (source, tests, captured test output) BEFORE hygiene runs. Ordering is essential: finalize-clean-tree.sh runs 'git clean -fdx' first, so any still-untracked deliverable would be deleted if it were not committed here. Idempotent: a no-op on a retry.

Deterministic working-tree hygiene (see scripts/finalize-clean-tree.sh, copied to agent/workspace/ by setup.sh): git clean -fdx of all untracked non-deliverable noise, an idempotent commit of any leftover tracked changes, then a porcelain gate. Lives one level above the repo so it never dirties the tree it cleans. Reused verbatim for every assignment; no deliverable-specific logic.

params.message: FINALIZE complete for task {{taskId}} -- working tree clean

DONE.name: Complete
DONE.description: Terminal state. The assignment is developed, tested, committed, and the working tree is clean.

ESCALATED.name: Escalated
ESCALATED.description: Terminal state reached when DEVELOP exhausts its retry budget (its onFailure self-loop is bounded by maxRetries). The assignment could not be completed deterministically and is left for human review.
